title: Term 3 Enrolments Now Open
summary: New semester commenced mid-July. New students welcome — come try a class for free! Over 383 registered members and growing.
content:
Term 3 has officially begun! Our new semester runs from mid-July to late September, aligned with the SA public school calendar.

We're thrilled to welcome both returning and new members. With over 383 registered members, our community continues to grow rapidly.

New this term: Senior Fitness program with Ms. Lina, and expanded Smartphone Classes.

Not sure which class to join? You're welcome to attend a trial session — just contact us via WeChat or fill in the enrolment form.

title: Senior Fitness Program Launch
summary: Introducing our new gentle exercise program with Ms. Lina — focused on stretching, mobility, and fall prevention for seniors.
content:
We're excited to launch our brand new Senior Fitness program, led by the wonderful Ms. Lina.

This gentle exercise program is specifically designed for older adults, focusing on:

  • Stretching and flexibility
  • Mobility improvement
  • Fall prevention techniques
  • Balance training

No prior fitness experience needed — all movements are adapted to individual abilities. Join us and stay active in a safe, supportive environment!

title: Mid-Autumn Festival Preparation
summary: Planning underway for our annual Mid-Autumn celebration. Performers and volunteers wanted — sign up now!
content:
Preparations are in full swing for our annual Mid-Autumn Festival celebration — one of the biggest events on our calendar!

We're looking for:

  • Performers (dance, music, poetry)
  • Event volunteers (setup, food, photography)
  • Mooncake donations and sponsors

This is always a highlight of the year — a wonderful opportunity to celebrate Chinese culture with our community. Contact us on WeChat to get involved!

title: End of Term 2 Celebrations
summary: Thank you to all students and instructors for a wonderful Term 2. Over 180 members attended the end-of-term gathering at 168 South Terrace.
content:
Term 2 concluded with a fantastic celebration at our 168 South Terrace venue. Over 180 members attended — our biggest end-of-term gathering yet!

Highlights included:

  • Vocal and Hulusi student performances
  • Photo slideshow of term highlights
  • Shared lunch and tea
  • Recognition of volunteer contributions

Thank you to every student, instructor, and volunteer who made Term 2 so special. We'll see you after the break!

title: New Photography Class Added
summary: We've introduced a photography class on Tuesdays with Mr. Xu Jiashu. All skill levels welcome — bring your camera or smartphone!
content:
Say cheese! We're delighted to introduce our newest program: Photography with Mr. Xu Jiashu.

This class is designed for absolute beginners through to hobbyists who want to improve their skills. Mr. Xu covers:

  • Camera and smartphone photography basics
  • Composition and lighting
  • Portrait and landscape techniques
  • Basic photo editing

Classes run every Tuesday 10:00–11:00 AM at 294–300 Morphett Street, Adelaide. Bring any camera — even your phone!

title: Harmony Week Performance
summary: Our dance and music groups performed at the SA Harmony Week celebrations — a proud moment for our multicultural community.
content:
What a proud moment for Adelaide Chinese Senior College! Our square dancing and vocal music groups were invited to perform at the South Australian Harmony Week celebrations.

Harmony Week is a time to celebrate Australia's cultural diversity, and our members represented the Chinese-Australian community with energy, passion and grace.

The audience loved the performances, and many attendees expressed interest in joining our programs. This is exactly the kind of community engagement and cross-cultural connection we strive for.

Well done to everyone involved!

title: Parliament House Visit
summary: Members visited South Australian Parliament House for a guided tour and meeting with local representatives.
content:
In March 2026, a group of our members visited the South Australian Parliament House for a guided tour and community engagement session.

The visit included:

  • A tour of the historic Parliament building
  • Meeting with local MPs
  • Learning about how laws are made in SA
  • Afternoon tea in the Parliamentary dining room

This was a wonderful opportunity for our members to engage with Australian democracy and civic life. We look forward to more such visits in the future!

title: Lunar New Year Celebration
summary: Over 100 members celebrated the Year of the Horse with performances, food, and festivities at 168 South Terrace.
content:
We rang in the Year of the Horse with a spectacular Lunar New Year celebration at our 168 South Terrace venue.

Over 100 members attended, enjoying:

  • Chinese dance performances
  • Hulusi and vocal music
  • Traditional New Year foods
  • Red envelope (hongbao) exchange
  • Calligraphy demonstrations

It was a joyful day of cultural celebration and community connection. Wishing everyone a healthy and prosperous Year of the Horse!
